scope: PliegoRS G3 PBOC, native/OCI, and Cloudflare deployment preview
disclaimer: This is a framework ownership map, not an application compliance claim.

controls.scopeNote: The sealed PBOC router uses the same canonical path grammar and route digest on native and Cloudflare hosts, with differential 404 and 405 cases.
The provider corpus preserves X-Content-Type-Options across hosts; applications remain responsible for applying the required response policy to every route.
PBOC declares host capability and route identity but does not grant trust to forwarded headers; proxy topology and trusted hops remain explicit operator policy.
Native Hyper and Cloudflare retain their own HTTP parsers while the public conformance corpus requires equal method, route, status, body, and relevant header semantics.
PBOC distinguishes public, private, session, and request cache domains; the G3 corpus verifies public policies while private production adapters remain application and operator work.
Every PBOC target references an exact artifact ledger plus committed SBOM and provenance paths, and bundle verification rejects missing, additional, modified, or linked files.
Host profiles declare bounded per-artifact and total upload sizes, and admission rejects unsupported required features before provider upload begins.
The G3 gate exercises exact-set admission, ordered streaming, immutable assets, rolling coexistence, rollback, OCI read-only operation, and negative feature negotiation.
PBOC declares bounded telemetry hooks and redacted fields, while exporter destination, access, retention, sampling, and alerting remain operator decisions.
Portable output contains secret identifiers and purposes only; a recursive sentinel scan and closed schema reject provider credentials and undeclared control-plane fields.
Admission and compatibility failures emit stable PBOC codes and receipts; correlating provider-side transport failures still depends on the selected operator telemetry.
The Cloudflare adapter exposes bounded public codes for routing and host failures while detailed internal errors remain restricted to operator diagnostics.
Unknown required features, route or runtime digest skew, invalid release chains, unsafe rollback, tampered artifacts, and secret leakage all fail closed in the maintained corpus.
